title: VAPT Assessment Pack
service_description: Comprehensive Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing service covering web application, API, network, and business logic security. Follows OWASP ASVS, WSTG, API Security Top 10, PTES, and NIST frameworks to deliver actionable findings with severity-graded remediation guidance.

intake_questions.text: List all in-scope IP ranges, domains, and applications
What is the authentication model (SSO, OAuth2, API keys, basic auth)?
Are there any WAF, IDS/IPS, or rate-limiting controls in front of targets?
What is the testing window (start datetime, end datetime, timezone)?
Are staging or production systems in scope? Who is the emergency contact?

scope_template.prompt: Add each in-scope domain or IP range
Define what is explicitly in scope
List systems or paths explicitly excluded from testing
Document agreed testing depth and credential access levels
Record rules of engagement, rate-limit constraints, and notification requirements

responsibilities: Own overall VAPT engagement delivery and rules of engagement
Lead reconnaissance and high-complexity exploit validation
Review and approve all findings before client sharing
Execute test phases 01–07 per the task plan
Document findings with PoC evidence and CVSS scores
Validate finding severity and evidence quality
Confirm QA gates G4 and G5 before report release
Confirm scope and rules of engagement
Provide emergency contact availability during testing window
Review draft findings and initiate remediation

requirements.text: Perform passive and active information gathering: DNS enumeration, WHOIS, certificate transparency, service fingerprinting.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Recon output (dns.txt, cert_transparency.txt, nmap service scan)

requirements.text: Review server and application configuration: HTTP security headers, default credentials, unnecessary services, directory listing.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Security headers report; nikto or automated scanner output

requirements.text: Review TLS/SSL configuration: protocol versions, cipher suites, certificate validity, and HSTS.
requirements.evidence_expectation: testssl.sh or SSLyze output; certificate details

requirements.text: Test authentication mechanisms: brute-force protection, lockout policy, credential stuffing resilience, MFA bypass.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Authentication test results; login attempt logs; MFA bypass test evidence

requirements.text: Test password reset and account recovery flows for insecure token generation, predictability, and expiry enforcement.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Password reset flow screenshots; token entropy analysis

requirements.text: Test horizontal and vertical privilege escalation: IDOR, missing function-level access control, and role boundary enforcement.
requirements.evidence_expectation: IDOR test cases with results; role-based access control verification evidence

requirements.text: Test for injection vulnerabilities: SQL injection, command injection, LDAP injection, and template injection.
requirements.evidence_expectation: SQLMap/manual injection test output; affected endpoints and payloads

requirements.text: Test for cross-site scripting (Reflected, Stored, DOM-based XSS) and HTML injection.
requirements.evidence_expectation: XSS test results; affected parameters; proof-of-concept payloads

requirements.text: Test session token entropy, fixation, hijacking, and proper invalidation on logout and timeout.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Session token analysis (Burp sequencer or similar); logout/timeout test results

requirements.text: Test API authentication and authorization: broken object-level auth (BOLA/IDOR), broken function-level auth (BFLA), mass assignment.
requirements.evidence_expectation: API test results with endpoint inventory; BOLA and BFLA test evidence

requirements.text: Test API for rate limiting, excessive data exposure, and security misconfiguration.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Rate limiting test results; data exposure findings; API schema review notes

requirements.text: Test business logic flows for abuse: price manipulation, workflow skipping, negative value inputs, and race conditions.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Business logic test cases and outcomes; specific workflow abuse scenarios

evidence_requests.description: Automated scanner report and manual configuration review notes

evidence_requests.description: testssl.sh or SSLyze full output for all in-scope endpoints

evidence_requests.description: Brute-force, lockout, and MFA bypass test evidence

evidence_requests.title: Injection test results
evidence_requests.description: SQL, command, and template injection findings with payloads

evidence_requests.title: API authorization test results

evidence_requests.description: Rate limiting test results; excessive data exposure findings

evidence_requests.title: Business logic test cases
evidence_requests.description: Documented abuse scenarios and test outcomes

assessment_procedures.title: Reconnaissance
steps: Conduct passive OSINT: WHOIS, DNS records, certificate transparency logs
Run active nmap service and version scan against all in-scope IP ranges
Enumerate subdomains via brute-force and zone transfer attempts
Fingerprint web server, framework, and CMS versions

assessment_procedures.title: Configuration and TLS review
steps: Run nikto or similar automated scanner against web targets
Test HTTP security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.)
Run testssl.sh against all HTTPS endpoints
Verify no default credentials on any in-scope service

assessment_procedures.title: Authentication and session testing
steps: Test for account lockout after N failed attempts
Attempt brute-force with common credential lists
Analyse session token entropy using Burp Sequencer
Test session invalidation on logout and timeout
Attempt session fixation by setting a known token before authentication

assessment_procedures.title: Authorisation testing
steps: Map all object-referencing endpoints (GUIDs, IDs, usernames)
Test IDOR by accessing resources with a different authenticated user
Test vertical privilege escalation by calling privileged endpoints as a low-privilege user
Verify role-based access control enforcement at the API layer

assessment_procedures.title: Input validation and injection
steps: Fuzz all input parameters with SQL injection payloads (manual + SQLMap)
Test for command injection on parameters that invoke OS commands
Test XSS on reflected, stored, and DOM-based injection points
Test for SSTI using template engine polyglot payloads

assessment_procedures.title: API security testing
steps: Enumerate API endpoints from documentation, JS sources, and traffic analysis
Test BOLA/IDOR for every object-referencing endpoint
Test BFLA by invoking admin functions as a standard user
Confirm rate limiting on authentication and sensitive endpoints
Test for mass assignment on POST/PUT/PATCH endpoints

task_templates.title: Kick-off call — scope confirmation and rules of engagement
Phase 04 — Authorisation and access control testing
Phase 05 — Input validation and injection testing
Retest of critical and high findings

severity_model.critical: Remote code execution, authentication bypass, or full data exfiltration with no user interaction
severity_model.high: Significant impact (SQLi, stored XSS, IDOR exposing sensitive data, BFLA to admin functions)
severity_model.medium: Limited impact requiring user interaction or specific conditions (reflected XSS, weak session tokens)
severity_model.low: Informational or low-impact issues (missing headers, verbose error messages)
severity_model.informational: Best-practice observations with no exploitable attack vector in current context

advisory_clinic_templates.title: Scope & Rules of Engagement Clinic
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Review confirmed scope assets, exclusions, and testing window. Confirm emergency contact, safe-harbour clause, and rate-limit constraints with the client team.

advisory_clinic_templates.title: Critical and High Findings Walkthrough
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Demonstrate critical and high findings to the technical team. Walk through exploitation steps, business impact, and recommended remediation for each finding.

advisory_clinic_templates.title: Remediation Planning Clinic
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Prioritise findings by severity and exploitability. Assign remediation owners, agree target dates, and define acceptance criteria for retest. Identify quick wins vs. long-term fixes.
